title: Introduction to Amazon Simple Storage Service (S3)

tasks.title: Task 1: Sign in to AWS Management Console

steps.text: Click on the Open Console button, and you will get redirected to AWS Console in a new browser tab.
On the AWS sign-in page,
Leave the Account ID as default. Never edit/remove the 12 digit Account ID present in the AWS Console. otherwise, you cannot proceed with the lab.
Now copy your User Name and Password in the Lab Console to the IAM Username and Password in AWS Console and click on the Sign in button.
Once Signed In to the AWS Management Console, Make the default AWS Region as US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1.
In this task, we are going to create a S3 bucket by providing the required configurations such as name, region and ACLs.
Ensure you are in the US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1 Region to begin creating an S3 bucket in the Amazon cloud.
Navigate to the Services menu at the top. Click on S3 in the Storage section and click on Create Bucket.
Task 2: Creating an S3 Bucket

On the Create Bucket page, fill in the bucket details. Select Bucket Type : General purpose Bucket name: Enter Unique name of your choice Note: Ensure you provide a unique bucket name of your choice, as S3 bucket names must be globally unique. For Object ownership: Select ACLs enabled Object ownership option: Choose Object writer
On the Create Bucket page, fill in the bucket details.
Select Bucket Type : General purpose
Bucket name: Enter Unique name of your choice Note: Ensure you provide a unique bucket name of your choice, as S3 bucket names must be globally unique.
Bucket name: Enter Unique name of your choice
Note: Ensure you provide a unique bucket name of your choice, as S3 bucket names must be globally unique.
Task 2: Creating an S3 Bucket
For Object ownership: Select ACLs enabled
Object ownership option: Choose Object writer
Task 2: Creating an S3 Bucket
Note: Please select Object owner, or else it won't allow editing the bucket ACL.

For Block Public Access settings for this bucket for this bucket section, Uncheck the option, Block all public access, Check the I acknowledge that the current settings might result in this bucket and the objects within becoming public checkbox.
For Block Public Access settings for this bucket for this bucket section,
Uncheck the option, Block all public access, Check the I acknowledge that the current settings might result in this bucket and the objects within becoming public checkbox.
Uncheck the option, Block all public access,
Check the I acknowledge that the current settings might result in this bucket and the objects within becoming public checkbox.
Task 2: Creating an S3 Bucket
Leave other settings as default.
Click on Create bucket button.
Kindly ignore the permission error if it appears during the process.
Task 2: Creating an S3 Bucket
The S3 bucket will be created successfully.

tasks.title: Task 3: Upload an object to S3 bucket

steps.text: Click on your bucket name.
In the Overview, You can see the following message: You don't have any objects in this bucket.
In the Overview, You can see the following message:
You don't have any objects in this bucket.
Task 3: Upload an object to S3 bucket
You can upload any image from your local machine or download the image from this Link.

To upload a file to our S3 bucket, Click on the Upload button. Click on Add files button. Browse for your local image or the image we provided and select it. Click on the Upload button. You can watch the progress of the upload from within the transfer panel at the top of the screen. Once your file has been uploaded, it will be displayed in the bucket.
To upload a file to our S3 bucket,
Click on the Upload button.
Click on Add files button.
Browse for your local image or the image we provided and select it.
Click on the Upload button.
You can watch the progress of the upload from within the transfer panel at the top of the screen.
Once your file has been uploaded, it will be displayed in the bucket.
Task 3: Upload an object to S3 bucket
Now click on the Close button on the top right corner of the screen.
In this task, we are going to change the permissions of the bucket to make the image publicly available.
Under Objects, Click on smiley.jpg, You will see image details like owner, size, link, etc.
A URL will be listed under the Object URL
Copy the Object URL and paste it in the browser in a new tab. You will see an AccessDenied message, which means the object is not publicly accessible.
Copy the Object URL and paste it in the browser in a new tab.
You will see an AccessDenied message, which means the object is not publicly accessible.
Task 4: Change Bucket Permissions
Go to your smiley.jpg object and navigate to the Permissions tab.
Task 4: Change Bucket Permissions
Now click on the Edit button on the right side.
Note: If the Edit button is disabled, please change the bucket ownership to ACLs enabled and choose the Object writer as the owner.
Everyone (public access) : Check the Read checkbox under Objects column.
Now scroll a little bit below and check I understand the effects of these changes on this object checkbox.
Task 4: Change Bucket Permissions
Now, Scroll to the bottom and click on Save changes button.
Now again paste the Object URL in the browser and you can see the image you have uploaded.
Task 4: Change Bucket Permissions
In the previous step, you granted read access only to a specific object. If you wish to make all objects inside a bucket available publicly, you can achieve this by creating a Bucket policy.
Go to the bucket by clicking on your bucket name - mys3bucketlab on the top.
Task 5: Create a Bucket Policy

Click the Permissions tab, then configure the following Scroll down to Bucket policy, click on Edit button on the Right side. A blank Bucket policy editor is displayed. Copy the ARN of your bucket to the clipboard. arn:aws:s3:::mys3bucketlab-test
Click the Permissions tab, then configure the following
Scroll down to Bucket policy, click on Edit button on the Right side.
A blank Bucket policy editor is displayed.
Copy the ARN of your bucket to the clipboard. arn:aws:s3:::mys3bucketlab-test
Copy the ARN of your bucket to the clipboard.
arn:aws:s3:::mys3bucketlab-test
Copy the entire policy, paste it into the bucket policy, and replace your bucket ARN with the ARN listed in the JSON below.
Task 5: Create a Bucket Policy
Click on Save changes button.

Go to the bucket and upload another image; download the lab logo image from Download Me Click on the Upload button. Click on Add files button. Browse for the downloaded image we provided and select it. Click on the Upload button.
Go to the bucket and upload another image; download the lab logo image from Download Me
Click on the Upload button.
Click on Add files button.
Browse for the downloaded image we provided and select it.
Click on the Upload button.
Once the image is uploaded successfully, click on the object name (Image name) , copy the Object URL, and open it in a browser. https://labresources.s3.amazonaws.com/lab-logo.png
Once the image is uploaded successfully, click on the object name (Image name) , copy the Object URL, and open it in a browser.
https://labresources.s3.amazonaws.com/lab-logo.png
Task 6: Test Public Access
You can see your image loaded successfully and is publicly accessible.

S3 has a built-in versioning feature that enables users to store multiple versions of an object within a bucket. This functionality is valuable for tracking changes and recovering previous versions of files in case of accidental deletions or data corruption.
Once the lab steps are completed, please click on the Validation button on the left side panel.
This will validate the resources in the AWS account and displays whether you have completed this lab successfully or not.
Sample output :
Task 7: Validation Test
You have successfully created a new AWS S3 Bucket.
You have successfully uploaded an image to the S3 bucket.
You have learned how to change S3 object permissions.
You have learned how to create an S3 bucket policy.
You have successfully validated the lab.
Sign out from the AWS Management Console.
Click on End Lab button from the lab console and wait till the process gets completed
